essence: Protects natural habitats and wildlife by managing conservation projects, monitoring ecosystems, and guiding sustainable land use.

identity.title: Conservation Officer
identity.summary: Works to protect wildlife and habitats through field surveys, conservation planning, community engagement, and environmental management.

salary.notes: Senior conservation officers, project leads, or specialists in biodiversity strategy can exceed £52k-£65k+.

qualifications: Training in conservation, ecology, or environmental science
Knowledge of habitat management and biodiversity protection
Ability to conduct ecological surveys and conservation planning
Understanding of environmental legislation and protected species

lifestyle.description: Conservation officers work across nature reserves, woodlands, wetlands, coastal areas, and community projects. They manage habitats, monitor wildlife, engage with landowners, and deliver conservation initiatives. The role suits individuals who enjoy fieldwork, ecology, community engagement, and environmental stewardship.

habits: Engages with landowners, volunteers, and communities
